threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it’s a good idea to take the tube to London Fields instead of just walking through Victoria Park. Classic rookie mistake.
Caught a night bus home from Brixton last week; a guy serenaded the whole back row with '80s power ballads. Best part of the trip.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw my neighbor arguing passionately with a pigeon over a bagel on the corner of Flughafenstraße, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a spontaneous rooftop cinema screening among the graffiti, where even the city's hustle pauses for a random movie night.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they have a favorite taco spot that isn’t on Yelp. The best ones are hidden behind a laundromat or in a garage.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today where the driver serenaded us with opera. Only in SF do you get a free concert while commuting.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all trendy bars, but miss the quiet corners where the real locals debate everything from fútbol to politics.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they struggle with the metro ticket machines; most locals know to avoid the 'help' button.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the Tube instead of the Overground, and your face lights up at the sight of the 'unique' graffiti art near the station.
Caught a guy on the 333 rapping to himself while the bus swayed; bus driver seemed to nod along, whole vibe was surprisingly uplifting.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The smell of fresh currywurst mingling with the sound of late-night techno from Späti parties—only in Neukölln do your senses collide like that.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a park where locals turn up with grills and disco music, claiming the sunset as theirs while sipping on Berliner Weisse.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to avoid the BART escalators during rush hour—always breaks down when the city's melting down with delays.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy playing a full drum kit on the 38 Geary this morning. Only in San Francisco do you get a live show with your commute.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always assume our bar terraces are for sunbathing. Most locals avoid them in summer; it’s too hot to enjoy a beer in the sun.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a recent arrival by their enthusiasm for the 'best crepes' in Gràcia—it's usually just a tourist trap with long lines.
